To see the environment variables that are set in your system, you can use different commands depending on your operating system:

### On Unix/Linux/MacOS:
You can use the `printenv` command or the `env` command in the terminal:

```bash
printenv
```
or
```bash
env
```

### On Windows:
You can use the `set` command in the Command Prompt:

```cmd
set
```

Or, if you are using PowerShell, you can use:

```powershell
Get-ChildItem Env:
```
